description: JSON texts that every port's fromJson MUST reject with its own clean parse error — never a crash, panic, silent acceptance, or a coerced value. These are the grammar edges where hand-rolled and library parsers historically diverged (HARDENING.md Item 4). One policy per edge (§4): duplicate keys, lone/unpaired surrogates, out-of-range floats, and non-JSON tokens are all rejected. Each port's conformance runner loads this list and asserts fromJson(text) fails.

cases.reason: duplicate object key (canonicality: a struple map can't hold two 'k' entries)
duplicate key even with equal values
lone high surrogate — not valid Unicode
lone low surrogate — not valid Unicode
high surrogate not followed by a low surrogate
number out of f64 range (parses to +inf)
number out of f64 range (parses to -inf)
